task: Launch-quality advanced task: Radiology Protocol Backlog Triage. Reality context: a radiology department has a protocoling backlog after EHR downtime. Embedded packet: metric map: metric1=study_age_hours, metric2=contrast_risk_score, metric3=clinical_urgency, metric4=radiologist_minutes; records: R1 study_age_hours=30 contrast_risk_score=4.39 clinical_urgency=60.28 radiologist_minutes=2; R2 study_age_hours=25 contrast_risk_score=7.04 clinical_urgency=46.51 radiologist_minutes=146; R3 study_age_hours=11 contrast_risk_score=2.48 clinical_urgency=51.53 radiologist_minutes=177; R4 study_age_hours=89 contrast_risk_score=7.59 clinical_urgency=83.21 radiologist_minutes=41; limit_study_age_hours=7; limit_contrast_risk_score=0.55; limit_clinical_urgency=52.55; decision_budget_usd=227500; sensitivity_shift_pct=5; hard_threshold_record_count=1; removed_context="benchmark framing favored long-horizon professional work with concrete verification". Rules and constraints: Fit a simple linear or monotone relationship from the embedded rows only; flag records whose residual exceeds the supplied tolerance or reverses expected direction. Options or recommendations cannot use external measurements, specialized licensed software, or hidden assumptions; all calculations must use only the embedded packet, and any contradictory or under-supported record must be marked needs_review. Required work: triage studies under staff constraints; convert the packet into an auditable evidence table, cite the governing values, and list unresolved assumptions. Deliver protocol_backlog_triage.csv + operations_memo.md with formulas or executable logic, record-level citations, validation checks, sensitivity notes, and a final recommendation table suitable for expert review.
